label: Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models

node.label: Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models
node.definition: Normalizing flows use invertible transformations with tractable density; rectified and flow-matching models instead learn continuous transport vector fields.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models is to connect Continuous normalizing flows & learned vector fields, Flow matching & transport objectives to downstream capabilities including Image generation, editing & design, Video generation & simulation synthesis, Weather, climate & geospatial forecasting.
examples: substrate for Image generation, editing & design
substrate for Video generation & simulation synthesis
substrate for Weather, climate & geospatial forecasting
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Continuous normalizing flows & learned vector fields, Flow matching & transport objectives.

dependencies.rationale: Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Continuous normalizing flows & learned vector fields.
Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Flow matching & transport objectives.
Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Differentiable ODE/PDE/SDE solvers.
